item.text: I’ll use `orbit tool run orbit.graph.*` from the repo root to query the knowledge graph for `ScopeStrategy::...` usages, then I’ll verify each reported file via tool output before listing results.

item.aggregated_output:
Execute a tool

Usage: orbit tool run [OPTIONS] <NAME>

Arguments:
  <NAME>  Tool name

Options:
      --input <INPUT>            JSON input for the tool (use --input-file to avoid shell escaping issues with rich content)
      --root <ROOT>              Override the Orbit root directory (highest precedence)
      --input-file <INPUT_FILE>  Path to a JSON file to use as input (bypasses shell escaping; preferred for markdown or multi-line content)
      --agent <AGENT>            Explicit agent name for provenance attribution (overrides ORBIT_AGENT_NAME)
      --model <MODEL>            Explicit agent model for provenance attribution (overrides ORBIT_AGENT_MODEL)
      --timeout <TIMEOUT>        Execution timeout (e.g. "30s", "5000ms")
      --dry-run                  Validate without executing
      --fields <FIELDS>          Comma-separated top-level fields to keep from object output
      --full                     Return the tool's full unfiltered JSON output
      --pretty                   Pretty-print JSON output for human debugging
      --output <OUTPUT>          Output format [default: json] [possible values: json, text]
  -h, --help                     Print help

Manage and run Orbit tools, including external MCP plugins

Usage: orbit tool [OPTIONS] <COMMAND>

Commands:
  list      List all registered tools
  show      Show detailed information about a tool
  run       Execute a tool
  add       Register an external tool or MCP plugin
  scaffold  Generate a starter external tool plugin
  remove    Remove an external tool
  enable    Enable a disabled tool
  disable   Disable a tool
  doctor    Validate tool health

Options:
      --root <ROOT>  Override the Orbit root directory (highest precedence)
  -h, --help         Print help

Examples:
  orbit tool scaffold ./plugins/hello_orbit.py --name demo.hello
  orbit tool add ./plugins/hello_orbit.py
  orbit tool show demo.hello

item.text: I hit a tooling friction point: `orbit tool show ...` failed with a readonly database write error. I’m going to probe whether `orbit tool run orbit.graph.*` itself still works in this environment; if not, I’ll report the blocker explicitly with evidence.

item.text: I now have the complete symbol-reference set from `orbit.graph.refs`; next I’m validating each referenced symbol’s source to keep only files that actually contain `ScopeStrategy::<Variant>` tokens.

item.command:
/bin/zsh -lc "selectors=(
  'symbol:crates/orbit-store/src/backend/file_backends.rs#TaskFileStore::strategy:method'
  'symbol:crates/orbit-store/src/backend/sqlite_backends.rs#SqliteAuditEventStoreBackend:impl'
  'symbol:crates/orbit-store/src/backend/sqlite_backends.rs#SqliteAuditEventStoreBackend::strategy:method'
  'symbol:crates/orbit-store/src/file/skill_store.rs#SkillCatalog:impl'
  'symbol:crates/orbit-store/src/file/skill_store.rs#SkillCatalog::strategy:method'
  'symbol:crates/orbit-store/src/scope.rs#ScopedStore:trait'
  'symbol:crates/orbit-store/src/scope.rs#resolve:function'
)

for s in \""'${selectors[@]}"; do
  out=$(orbit tool run orbit.graph.show --input "{'"\\\"selector\\\":\\\""'$s'"\\\"}\")
  file="'$(printf '"'%s' \""'$out" | jq -r '"'.lineage[] | select(startswith(\"file:\")) | sub(\""'^file:"; "")'"')
  src="'$(printf '"'%s' \""'$out" | jq -r '"'.source // \"\"')

  has=0
  for v in WorkspaceOnly GlobalOnly MergeByKey WorkspaceReplaces; do
    case \""'$src" in
      *"ScopeStrategy::$v"*) has=1 ;;
    esac
  done

  if [ "$has" -eq 1 ]; then
    printf '"'MATCH %s | %s\\n' \""'$file" "$s"
  else
    printf '"'NO_MATCH %s | %s\\n' \""'$file" "$s"
  fi
done'

/bin/zsh -lc "selectors=(
  'symbol:crates/orbit-store/src/backend/file_backends.rs#TaskFileStore::strategy:method'
  'symbol:crates/orbit-store/src/backend/sqlite_backends.rs#SqliteAuditEventStoreBackend:impl'
  'symbol:crates/orbit-store/src/backend/sqlite_backends.rs#SqliteAuditEventStoreBackend::strategy:method'
  'symbol:crates/orbit-store/src/file/skill_store.rs#SkillCatalog:impl'
  'symbol:crates/orbit-store/src/file/skill_store.rs#SkillCatalog::strategy:method'
  'symbol:crates/orbit-store/src/scope.rs#ScopedStore:trait'
  'symbol:crates/orbit-store/src/scope.rs#resolve:function'
)

for s in \""'${selectors[@]}"; do
  out=$(orbit tool run orbit.graph.show --input "{'"\\\"selector\\\":\\\""'$s'"\\\"}\")
  file="'$(printf '"'%s' \""'$out" | jq -r '"'.lineage[] | select(startswith(\"file:\")) | sub(\""'^file:"; "")'"')
  src="'$(printf '"'%s' \""'$out" | jq -r '"'.source // \"\"')

  has=0
  for v in WorkspaceOnly GlobalOnly MergeByKey WorkspaceReplaces; do
    case \""'$src" in
      *"ScopeStrategy::$v"*) has=1 ;;
    esac
  done

  if [ "$has" -eq 1 ]; then
    printf '"'MATCH %s | %s\\n' \""'$file" "$s"
  else
    printf '"'NO_MATCH %s | %s\\n' \""'$file" "$s"
  fi
done'

item.text: I found the earlier loop issue: some invocations are returning `store error: attempt to write a readonly database`. I’m re-running with static single-quoted inputs per selector to avoid interpolation artifacts and confirm the true file set.
